question:
[Previous Feedback] Great! The log_utils.py file is well-structured with proper documentation and the format_log_entry function. The example demonstrates the correct datetime format.

Please create day04/api_versions.json to record API version history: v2.1.0 (released 2026-01-15), v2.2.0 (released 2026-02-28), v2.3.0 (released today 2026-03-19). Each version should include version, released_at (release time convention 12:00), and deprecated_at (null indicates not deprecated) fields.

content.text:
[Wed 2026-03-18 12:39 EDT] [Previous Feedback] Great! The log_utils.py file is well-structured with proper documentation and the format_log_entry function. The example demonstrates the correct datetime format.

Please create day04/api_versions.json to record API version history: v2.1.0 (released 2026-01-15), v2.2.0 (released 2026-02-28), v2.3.0 (released today 2026-03-19). Each version should include version, released_at (release time convention 12:00), and deprecated_at (null indicates not deprecated) fields.

Done! I've created `day04/api_versions.json` with the API version history.

**Version entries:**

| Version | Released At | Status |
|---------|-------------|--------|
| v2.1.0 | 2026-01-15T12:00:00+08:00 | Active (not deprecated) |
| v2.2.0 | 2026-02-28T12:00:00+08:00 | Active (not deprecated) |
| v2.3.0 | 2026-03-19T12:00:00+08:00 | Active (not deprecated) |

All `released_at` timestamps follow the standardized ISO 8601 format with 12:00 release time convention and `+08:00` timezone offset. The `deprecated_at` fields are set to `null` indicating all versions are currently active.
